sections.paragraph:
ἐλεύϑερος, α, ον [fr. a conjectured root shared by Lat. liber ‘free’] ‘enjoying freedom from constraint’, free, independent

—a. in general 1 Cor 9:1.

—b. of non-slave status J 8:33, 36; 1 Cor 9:19; 12:13; Gal 3:28; 4:22f, 30; Eph 6:8; Col 3:11; 1 Pt 2:16; Rv 6:15; 13:16; 19:18. Metaph. Ro 6:20; Gal 4:26. As noun ὁ ἐ. freedman, a term applied to freed slaves, who then became clients of their masters, metaph. 1 Cor 7:22.

—c. of marital constraint Ro 7:3; 1 Cor 7:39.

—d. of exclusion from taxation Mt 17:26.
